problem: Who received the IEEE Frank Rosenblatt Award in 2010?

response.answer: Michio Sugeno received the IEEE Frank Rosenblatt Award in 2010.
search_results: https://ethw.org/IEEE_Frank_Rosenblatt_Award: "Michio Sugeno , 2010"

problem: Who was awarded the Oceanography Society's Jerlov Award in 2018?

response.answer: The Oceanography Society's Jerlov Award in 2018 was awarded to Dr. Annick Bricaud. "The Oceanography Society is pleased to announce that Dr. Annick Bricaud has been selected as the 2018 recipient of The Nils Gunnar Jerlov Award recognizing her contributions to the advancement of our knowledge of the nature and consequences of light in the ocean. Dr. Bricaud is recognized internationally for her groundbreaking contributions to ocean optics, which cover experimental and theoretical studies on seawater optical properties, fieldwork-based bio-optical relationships, and algorithms for deriving biogeochemical products from satellite ocean color. She is a senior research scientist at the Laboratoire d’Océanographie de Villefranche (LOV), CNRS – Sorbonne Université, Villefranche-sur-Mer, France, which she joined in 1976. Her 75 papers received the outstanding number of 11,400 citations, and many opened new avenues of research. Annick mentored numerous students, in France and worldwide, and has a long record of contribution to community service. She is among the first female researchers in optical oceanography."
search_results: https://2018.oceanopticsconference.org/jerlov-award/: "The Oceanography Society is pleased to announce that Dr. Annick Bricaud has been selected as the 2018 recipient of The Nils Gunnar Jerlov Award recognizing her contributions to the advancement of our knowledge of the nature and consequences of light in the ocean. Dr. Bricaud is recognized internationally for her groundbreaking contributions to ocean optics, which cover experimental and theoretical studies on seawater optical properties, fieldwork-based bio-optical relationships, and algorithms for deriving biogeochemical products from satellite ocean color. She is a senior research scientist at the Laboratoire d’Océanographie de Villefranche (LOV), CNRS – Sorbonne Université, Villefranche-sur-Mer, France, which she joined in 1976. Her 75 papers received the outstanding number of 11,400 citations, and many opened new avenues of research. Annick mentored numerous students, in France and worldwide, and has a long record of contribution to community service. She is among the first female researchers in optical oceanography."

problem: What's the name of the women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts?

response.answer: The women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, is Radcliffe College. According to Wikipedia, "Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, that was founded in 1879. It was one of the Seven Sisters colleges and was fully incorporated into Harvard College in 1999."
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radcliffe_College: "Radcliffe College was a women's liberal arts college in Cambridge, Massachusetts, that was founded in 1879. It was one of the Seven Sisters colleges and was fully incorporated into Harvard College in 1999."

problem: In whose honor was the Leipzig 1877 tournament organized?

response.answer: The Leipzig 1877 tournament was organized in honor of Adolf Anderssen, celebrating his 50th anniversary of learning chess.
search_results: https://kids.kiddle.co/Adolf_Anderssen: "The Leipzig 1877 tournament was held to honor Anderssen. It was called the \"Anderssen-Feier\" (Anderssen Celebration). Anderssen finished second in this tournament, behind Louis Paulsen."

problem: According to Karl Küchler, what did Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture depict, which was made for her villa Achilleion at Corfu?

response.answer: According to Karl Küchler, Empress Elizabeth of Austria's favorite sculpture made for her villa Achilleion at Corfu depicted Achilles. The garden of the Achilleion palace features a famous statue of 9Achilles the Immortal9 by the sculptor Ernest Herter.
search_results: https://www.travel.gr/en/experiences-ee/travel-en/corfu-town-guide/: "The garden also features a famous statue of 9Achilles the Immortal9 by the sculptor Ernest Herter. The palace has since been restored to its former glory and is used for cultural events."

problem: How much money, in euros, was the surgeon held responsible for Stella Obasanjo's death ordered to pay her son?

response.answer: The surgeon held responsible for Stella Obasanjo's death was ordered to pay $176,000 in compensation to her son.
search_results: https://www.abc.net.au/news/2009-09-22/doctor-jailed-over-former-first-ladys-lipo-death/1437416: "A Spanish doctor has been sent to jail for a year over the death of the former Nigerian first lady. Stella Obasanjo died after having liposuction at a clinic in Marbella four years ago. The court heard that a tube used for removing fat had been placed by mistake into the patients abdominal cavity, puncturing her colon and lacerating her liver, causing her to fall seriously ill. The surgeon initially failed to answer his mobile phone and then waited more than four hours before driving the patient in his own car to an intensive care unit where she died. search_results: https://www.huffpost.com/entry/obama-i-believe-in-the-re_n_83208: "In a recent interview with Christianity Today, presidential candidate Barack Obama shared his religious beliefs, and answered questions on questions central to evangelical interests, such as abortion. \"I am a Christian, and I am a devout Christian. I believe in the redemptive death and resurrection of Jesus Christ.\"

problem: Who appointed the Chief Justice of India, Mirza Hameedullah Beg, in 1977?

response.answer: Mirza Hameedullah Beg was appointed Chief Justice of India in 1977 by the Indira Gandhi government.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mirza_Hameedullah_Beg: "A few months later, in January 1977, M.H. Beg, who was junior to H. R. Khanna, was appointed Chief Justice of India by the Indira Gandhi government."

problem: What is the name of the former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971?

response.answer: The former Prime Minister of Iceland who worked as a cabin crew member until 1971 is Jóhanna Sigurðardóttir.
search_results: https://www.britannica.com/biography/Johanna-Sigurdardottir: "Sigurðardóttir worked as a flight attendant for Loftleiðir Icelandic Airlines from 1962 to 1971, and she was an active labour union member, twice serving as chairman of the board of the Icelandic Cabin Crew Association (1966, 1969)."

problem: To whom did Mehbooba Mufti Sayed contest the 2019 Lok Sabha elections and lose?

response.answer: Mehbooba Mufti Sayed contested the 2019 Lok Sabha elections from the Anantnag-Rajouri seat and lost to National Conference candidate Mian Altaf Ahmad. She lost by 181,000 votes.
search_results: https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/elections/lok-sabha-constituencies/jammu-kashmir/anantnag: "In 2014 Lok Sabha elections, Mehbooba Mufti of JKPDP had won the seat. The 2019 Parliamentary elections witnessed a voter turnout of 9.7% in the constituency."

https://www.hindustantimes.com/india-news/pdp-suffers-worst-electoral-result-since-its-formation-25-years-back-101728374957059.html: "Mehbooba Mufti lost by 181,000 votes from Anantnag-Rajouri Lok Sabha seat."

problem: In which year did Melbourne's Monash Gallery of Art (MGA) rebrand and become the Museum of Australian Photography (MAPh)?

response.answer: Melbourne's Monash Gallery of Art (MGA) rebranded and became the Museum of Australian Photography (MAPh) in the year 2023, with the official launch of the rebrand on 19 March 2023.
search_results: https://www.australianphotography.com/news/monash-gallery-of-art-to-rebrand-as-museum-of-australian-photography: "Melbourne's Monash Gallery of Art (MGA) is set to rebrand and become known as the Museum of Australian Photography (MAPh) from later this month... MAPh will officially launch its rebrand on Sunday 19 March 2023 at 12:00pm"

problem: Who requested the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) implement a 900 sq mi (2,300 km2) temporary flight restriction zone over the operations areas of the Deepwater Horizon?

response.answer: The Coast Guard requested the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to implement a 900 sq mi (2,300 km2) temporary flight restriction zone over the operations areas of the Deepwater Horizon.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deepwater_Horizon_oil_spill: "During the spill response operations, at the request of the Coast Guard, the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) implemented a 900 sq mi (2,300 km2) temporary flight restriction zone over the operations area."

problem: What signature piece of the MOBA did Scott Wilson discover on the curb between two trash cans?
answer: Lucy in the Field with Flowers

response.answer: Scott Wilson discovered the Museum of Bad Art's signature piece, "Lucy in the Field with Flowers," protruding from between two trash cans on a curb in the Roslindale area of Boston.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Museum_of_Bad_Art: "The Museum of Bad Art was founded by antique dealer Scott Wilson, who discovered what has become the museum's signature pieceLucy in the Field with Flowersprotruding from between two trash cans on a Roslindale-area curb in Boston, among some garbage waiting to be collected."

problem: What is the surname of the psychiatrist who prescribes medication for Marie Hanson for her periodic blackouts in Season 1, Episode 20 of Ally McBeal?

response.answer: The surname of the psychiatrist who prescribes medication for Marie Hanson for her periodic blackouts in Season 1, Episode 20 of Ally McBeal is Peters.
search_results: https://allymcbeal.fandom.com/wiki/The_Inmates: "Her psychiatrist, Dr. Peters, had prescribed her medication. Hanson tells the pair it is obvious she is the killer but she does not remember performing the act although she does recollect swinging the hatchet. Ally and Bobby meet with Hanson's psychiatrist, Dr. Peters. He believes Hanson may suffer from some form of neurological disorder and may have committed the murder during one of her black-out periods which likens to sleepwalking."

problem: What position was John Gilbert Layton appointed to in Quebec from 1969 until 1970?

response.answer: John Gilbert Layton was appointed as the Quebec Youth Parliament prime minister from 1969 until 1970.
search_results: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_Layton: "In 1969, he was appointed as the Quebec Youth Parliament prime minister, holding this position until 1970."
